symptoms.symptom_name: Acute crop of hundreds of tense, deep-seated, clear-fluid vesicles on both plantar arches, intensely pruritic and burning

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A florid plantar vesiculobullous eruption is the classic inflammatory (id-type) presentation of tinea pedis, especially with T. interdigitale.
Deep-seated clear plantar vesicles with intense itch are also typical of pompholyx — the finding cannot separate the two without the other tinea features.

symptoms.symptom_name: Concurrent interdigital maceration, fissuring and peeling with chronic moccasin-pattern scaling along the foot borders

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Web-space maceration and moccasin scaling identify the underlying chronic dermatophytosis and are the key feature distinguishing a tinea flare from primary pompholyx.
Prominent interdigital maceration and chronic scaling are not features of a primary endogenous pompholyx and point away from it toward a dermatophyte cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: Trigger of several days' occlusive footwear (heavy boots, thick wool socks) with hyperhidrosis in hot conditions

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Warmth, moisture and occlusion promote dermatophyte proliferation and precipitate an inflammatory flare; also a modifiable driver targeted by anti-occlusion advice.
Prolonged contact with boots/socks keeps a footwear contact dermatitis on the differential, though the distribution favours tinea.

symptoms.symptom_name: Mild distal subungual nail thickening and yellow discolouration (onychomycosis), with foot malodour

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Distal subungual onychomycosis indicates an established dermatophyte reservoir on the feet, supporting a fungal aetiology for the acute flare.

symptoms.symptom_name: Deep-seated, tense vesicles with clear (serous, non-purulent) fluid on an erythematous base

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Tense, deep-seated serous vesicles argue against bullous impetigo (whose bullae are flaccid and rupture easily) and against pustular processes; confirming the serous, non-purulent character reliably needs in-person inspection and so is sealed as answer-key.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe burning pain on weight-bearing with functional immobility (crawling/using furniture)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Marks symptom severity and the functional impact that justifies prompt (but not emergency) management; the intensity of pain does not by itself raise the tier for a benign dermatosis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Afebrile and systemically well with no spreading erythema or ascending lymphangitis

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No fever, spreading warm erythema or lymphangitis argues strongly against cellulitis/systemic infection and keeps this a low-tier, non-urgent presentation; the patient can reliably report these, so history is sufficient.

symptoms.symptom_name: No thick yellow pus, no honey-coloured crusting

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of purulence and honey crusting argues against current impetiginisation/secondary infection and against pre-emptive antibiotics; the patient can report the fluid colour, though definitive assessment is clinician inspection.

symptoms.symptom_name: Skin scrapings for fungal microscopy (KOH) and dermatophyte culture

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Fungal microscopy and culture are the definitive way to confirm dermatophytosis and to distinguish it from pompholyx/contact dermatitis; unavailable in telehealth and the reason the diagnosis is presumptive and in-person confirmation is arranged. Not urgent.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Skin scrapings for fungal microscopy (KOH) and dermatophyte culture at in-person GP review
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms the dermatophyte diagnosis and separates tinea from pompholyx/contact dermatitis before or during a potent-steroid course; arranged at the routine/prompt in-person review, not urgently, and not performable via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person GP examination of the feet (vesicle character, web spaces, nails, exclusion of secondary infection)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms the clinical diagnosis, assesses the differential and safely governs the short potent-steroid prescription; appropriate within 24-48 hours given severity, but not an emergency.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Bacterial swab / wound culture if secondary infection develops
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Indicated only if purulence, honey crusting or spreading infection appears (safety-net trigger); not needed now given clear vesicles and an afebrile, well patient.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Routine bloods / inflammatory markers ordered remotely
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not indicated for a localised, benign, systemically-well inflammatory foot dermatosis; ordering bloods over telehealth here is unnecessary over-investigation.
